project.primary_goal: Make the owner's payday and envelope workflow easier without exposing financial data

requested_changes.outcome: Preview percentage-based savings allocations from a take-home paycheck and record them only after explicit approval
requested_changes.notes: Keep the planner deterministic and independent of banks or runtime AI services

environment.data_location_preference: An application-private directory selected during setup
environment.authentication_expectation: No additional authentication while the app is bound only to loopback
environment.notes: Reconsider authentication before changing the network boundary

data_posture.migration_scope: No existing ledger migration is part of this synthetic example
data_posture.notes: Use a newly bootstrapped fictional workspace for implementation and demonstration

appearance.notes: Calm, readable, and restrained in both light and dark modes
accessibility_requirements: Keep text and interactive control contrast at or above the project's validated thresholds

account_roles.purpose: Receives income and funds the payday plan
Holds savings intended for longer time horizons

workflows.trigger: The user is processing a take-home paycheck
inputs: Selected source and destination account roles
Accessible-savings target and current accessible balance
rules: Calculate consequential money values as integer cents and contribution percentages as basis points
Allocate 100% of a contribution to the quick-access reserve while its opening balance is below the target, even when that contribution reaches or crosses the target
Starting with the next contribution after the target has been reached, allocate 100% to the long-term destination
A preview never writes transactions, transfers, splits, or settings
workflows.review_point: Show each allocation, grouped transfer totals, and the remaining paycheck before recording anything
writes: Matching envelope splits for the approved transfer legs
workflows.recovery: Canceling before confirmation writes nothing; later correction follows the existing linked-transfer edit and reconciliation protections
workflows.privacy_demo: Use fictional institutions, account roles, balances, paycheck amounts, and transactions
workflows.success: The preview matches every deterministic rule and confirmation creates balanced linked transfers with matching splits

privacy.notes: Use fictional institutions, people, balances, and transactions

decisions.subject: Accessible-savings target routing
decisions.statement: A contribution begun below the target goes entirely to accessible savings, including a contribution that crosses the target; later contributions go entirely to long-term savings
decisions.consequence_if_wrong: The recommended destination and recorded transfer would not match the user's savings policy

decisions.subject: Network and authentication boundary
decisions.statement: This example is implemented and tested as a loopback-only local web application
decisions.consequence_if_wrong: A broader access boundary would require a separate authentication and deployment plan

acceptance_scenarios.given: A fictional $2,000 paycheck, a 10% savings rule, and an accessible balance below its target
acceptance_scenarios.when: The user previews the payday plan
acceptance_scenarios.then: The full $200 recommendation goes to accessible savings and no records are written

acceptance_scenarios.given: A fictional contribution starts with the accessible balance below target and is large enough to cross it
acceptance_scenarios.when: The user previews that contribution and then previews a later contribution
acceptance_scenarios.then: The crossing contribution goes entirely to accessible savings and the later contribution goes entirely to long-term savings

acceptance_scenarios.given: A reviewed fictional recommendation with compatible source, destination, and envelope roles
acceptance_scenarios.then: Balanced linked transfer legs and matching envelope splits are recorded atomically
financial_invariants: Splits sum exactly to their signed parent amounts

handoff_notes: Do not infer desktop or server deployment support from this local-web example
